capabilities.claim: Contract, Preflight, verification, Summary, archive, PR, close, and base-sync governance exists in this repository.
The template can inspect project facts and create a profile proposal; it does not prove completed adopter calibration.
An adopter receives the governed entrypoints when the installer completes; installed availability must be checked in the adopter repository.
The Runtime provides an adopter-executable, Japanese-default ten-stage calibration Session with pause/resume, review, checks, evidence, and fail-closed Candidate activation.
Calibration and readiness consumers share a conservative ten-boundary Inventory with explicit status, provenance, confirmation, staleAt, owner, and blocking reason fields.
Calibration Candidate activation is atomic and preserves the previous Active configuration when activation fails.
Bootstrap Adoption has an explicit resumable lifecycle schema, confirmation-gated write phase, and a fail-closed not_claimed readiness boundary.
An adopter receives a bound per-file ownership manifest with installed/current digests, project modification facts, managed-region evidence, and rollback baseline consumed by the installed lifecycle.

Installed update proposal/apply and preserve-evidence uninstall boundaries expose standard Work Item, snapshot, migration, recalibration, PR handoff, rollback, detached-uninstaller, purge-export, and receipt evidence.
Quick Install does not yet default to independently verified Release Archive SHA256 evidence.
capabilities.missingEvidence: A source-bound release archive digest check and clean/anonymous install output are not yet recorded.

capabilities.claim: Release and PR claims require independently bound provider Workflow Run, Head SHA, Required Job Names, Conclusions, failure reasons, and artifact evidence before being called verified.
Enterprise identity, authorization, isolation, immutable audit, and compliance assurance remain outside AI Cockpit evidence.
